economic_notes: Nation operates a retail gas bar through 102009262 Saskatchewan Ltd. COVID-19 impacted operations including office closures, travel restrictions, reduced hours. Future funding from First Nations Trust and Dakota Dunes Community Development Corporation expected to be negatively impacted due to temporary closure of SIGA casinos. Nation is party to a Management Action Plan with ISC due to certain deficiencies. Forensic Review by ISC identified $355,000 as potentially recoverable.
notes: Audit is qualified due to deficiencies in accounting records and internal controls of the retail gas bar (102009262 Saskatchewan Ltd.), preventing sufficient audit evidence on completeness of retail sales and appropriateness of expenses. Two wholly owned subsidiaries consolidated: Kinistin Bison Inc. and 102009262 Saskatchewan Ltd. Cost method investments in STC Investments Limited Partnership (14%) and STC Casino Holdings Corporation (14%) not consolidated. Agricultural implements claim loan of $708,939 is non-interest bearing advance from Crown to finance claim costs. ISC Forensic Review identified $355,000 potentially recoverable from Nation - disputed by Nation. Nation under Management Action Plan with ISC. COVID-19 noted as significant event. Fiscal year 2020 but audit signed April 23, 2021 - significant delay. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,851,061 + Health Canada $548,838 + CMHC $249,747 = $5,649,646. OSR includes STC Health and Family Services, Gas Bar Sales, Saskatoon Tribal Council, First Nations Trust, Miscellaneous, Dakota Dunes CDC, Rent, STC Casino Holdings, Livestock inventory adjustment, Pasture rental.
